catalog.name: atr_1
catalog.description: Relative 14-period ATR, expressed as ATR divided by close.

catalog.name: bars_since_expiry
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance since the most recent expiry/delivery event; 0 on the event bar and NaN before the first known event.

catalog.name: bars_since_funding_settlement
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance since the most recent funding settlement; 0 on the settlement bar and NaN before the first known settlement.

catalog.name: bars_until_expiry
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance until the next expiry/delivery event; 0 on the event bar and NaN after the last known future event.

catalog.name: bars_until_funding_settlement
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance until the next funding settlement; 0 on the settlement bar and NaN after the last known settlement.

catalog.name: cf_atr_regime
catalog.description: ATR regime detector: short/long ATR ratio to identify expanding vs contracting volatility regimes

catalog.name: cf_flow_cumulative_delta_1h
catalog.description: Running signed volume imbalance: cumulative delta over 6 bars on 1h, proxying buy vs sell pressure accumulation

catalog.name: cf_fund_diverge
catalog.description: Funding settlement proximity signal: contrarian momentum signal around funding settlement events using bars_until/bars_since funding settlement

catalog.name: cf_funding_extreme
catalog.description: Contrarian signal around funding settlement events: uses bars_until/bars_since funding settlement to identify pre/post settlement zones, combines with 8-bar return momentum for directional contrarian signals.

catalog.name: cf_funding_momentum_drift
catalog.description: Funding regime momentum overlay: combines existing funding proximity regime (cf_funding_regime) with 4/8-bar price momentum to create directional signals. Approaching funding with positive momentum → mean reversion short; negative → long bounce. Post-settlement follows momentum continuation.

catalog.name: cf_funding_regime
catalog.description: Funding settlement proximity regime: classifies bars into approaching_funding(1), neutral(2), post_settlement(3), at_settlement(4) based on bars_until/bars_since funding settlement.

catalog.name: cf_funding_settlement_gate
catalog.description: Funding settlement proximity gate: fires on settlement bars and the immediately following bar

catalog.name: cf_fwd_vol_regime
catalog.description: Forward 5-bar volatility regime classifier: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high. Uses rolling quantile-based thresholds on forward return volatility.

catalog.name: cf_mean_rev_pressure
catalog.description: Mean-reversion pressure: z-score of close vs 24h rolling VWAP, amplified by RSI divergence filter. Continuous value: positive=overbought pressure, negative=oversold. Divergence between price and RSI momentum amplifies the signal 1.5x.

catalog.name: cf_momentum_divergence_6_24
catalog.description: Cross-lookback momentum divergence: 6-bar ROC minus 24-bar ROC. Positive = short-term momentum accelerating relative to longer-term (trend strengthening). Negative = short-term momentum weakening relative to longer-term (trend exhaustion / transition signal). Captures regime transitions that single-timeframe anchors cannot detect.

catalog.name: cf_range_compression_1h
catalog.description: Ratio of 12-bar ATR to 48-bar ATR on 1h, detecting contraction before expansion moves. Values < 1 = compression, > 1 = expansion.

catalog.name: cf_rv_percentile_48
catalog.description: Rolling 48-bar percentile rank of realized variance — identifies regime extremes

catalog.name: cf_rv_ratio_6_24
catalog.description: Ratio of 6-bar realized variance to 24-bar realized variance on 1h — spikes above 1.5 signal volatility expansion

catalog.name: cf_vol_absorption_score
catalog.description: Volume absorption: volume z-score times (1 - body/range), smoothed over 6 bars. High values = large volume with small body = institutional absorption.

catalog.name: cf_vol_asymmetry_1h
catalog.description: Ratio of upside candle body range to total body range over rolling 6 bars on 1h, capturing directional vol skew from OHLCV alone. Values > 0 = upside dominance, < 0 = downside dominance.

catalog.name: cf_vol_expansion_flag
catalog.description: Vol expansion flag: binary 1 when 5-bar realized vol exceeds 20-bar vol by 1.5x, else 0. Captures sudden vol breakouts.

catalog.name: cf_vol_imbalance_12
catalog.description: Rolling 12-bar volume imbalance: (sum_up_vol - sum_down_vol) / total_vol on 5m bars

catalog.name: cf_vol_price_div
catalog.description: Volume-price divergence: signed rolling price-volume correlation, negative means price trends without volume confirmation

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_compression
catalog.description: 20-bar realized vol percentile rank vs 100-bar window; -1=compression (bottom decile), 1=expansion (top decile), 0=neutral

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_switch
catalog.description: Volatility regime switch detector: binary flag (0/1) based on ATR ratio of 6h vs 48h rolling windows. Value=1 when short-term vol deviates significantly from long-term (ratio >1.2 or <0.8), indicating regime transition.

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_transition
catalog.description: Vol regime transition: 20-bar realized vol percentile minus 5-bar realized vol percentile over 100-bar window. Positive = vol expanding, negative = vol contracting.

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_vov
catalog.description: Vol-of-vol ratio: 5-bar ATR std / 20-bar ATR std. Values > 1 indicate short-term vol accelerating relative to longer-term.

catalog.name: cf_vol_squeeze_breakout
catalog.description: Detects volatility squeeze release: ratio of 6-bar to 24-bar realized vol crosses above 0.65 after being below 0.55 for 3+ consecutive bars. Binary 1/0 signal.

catalog.name: cf_vol_term_spread
catalog.description: Volatility term-structure regime signal. Ratio of 12-period (1h) to 96-period (8h) rolling return std, minus 1. Values >0 = vol expansion (trending regime), <0 = vol contraction (mean-reverting regime). Captures regime TRANSITIONS, not levels — orthogonal to ATR and single-window vol features.

catalog.name: cf_vwap_dev
catalog.description: Percentage distance of close from 24-period rolling VWAP. Positive = price above VWAP, negative = price below VWAP. Captures mean-reversion regimes.

catalog.name: cf_zscore_reversion
catalog.description: Z-score mean-reversion: 24-bar rolling z-score of close price. Positive values = oversold (buy signal for mean-reversion), negative = overbought.

catalog.name: cl_15m
catalog.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 15m candlestick model. Class 0: long base then powerful upside impulse, sometimes in multiple steps; class 1: broad volatile sideways range with a downward bias; class 2: persistent decline through most of the window; class 3: sharp opening surge then hold at elevated levels with slight continued rise.

value_semantics.meaning: Long base then powerful upside impulse, sometimes in multiple steps.

catalog.name: cl_1d
catalog.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 1d candlestick model. Class 0: long flat base then sharp upside pump; class 1: monotonic downtrend with acceleration into a weak rebound; class 2: smooth rise into a local top then short pullback; class 3: strong uptrend that accelerates into the end; class 4: wide volatile range with a rounded top then decline.

value_semantics.meaning: Long flat base then sharp upside pump.
Monotonic downtrend with acceleration into a weak rebound.

catalog.name: cl_1h
catalog.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 1h candlestick model. Class 0: sideways to weak down drift followed by a sharp late selloff; class 1: strong early drop then weak bottom-side consolidation; class 2: fast rise in the first half then topping plateau or light pullback; class 3: steady upward acceleration through most of the window, sometimes with a final upside burst.

catalog.name: cl_4h
catalog.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 4h candlestick model. example_args.name: cf_micro_imbalance_1h
example_args.description: Aggregate 5m signed-volume imbalance into one 1h anchor-aligned series.
example_args.code:
import pandas as pd

from app.features.custom.lower_timeframe import aggregate_completed_lower_timeframe

def compute_series(ctx):
    frame = ctx.frame(timeframe='5m')[['open', 'high', 'low', 'close', 'volume', 'timestamp_utc']].copy()
    def _aggregate(lower_frame):
        indexed = lower_frame.set_index('timestamp_utc')
        rng = (indexed['high'] - indexed['low']).replace(0, pd.NA)
        signed_range = (indexed['close'] - indexed['open']) / rng
        signed_volume = signed_range * indexed['volume']
        hourly_signed = signed_volume.resample('1h', label='right', closed='left').sum()
        hourly_volume = indexed['volume'].resample('1h', label='right', closed='left').sum().replace(0, pd.NA)
        return hourly_signed / hourly_volume
    return aggregate_completed_lower_timeframe(
        anchor_frame=ctx.frame(),
        lower_frame=frame,
        lower_timeframe='5m',
        anchor_timeframe='1h',
        feature_name='cf_micro_imbalance_1h',
        aggregator=_aggregate,
    )

stepwise_access_rules: step.current/step.series/step.window read raw timeframe frame columns only.
step.feature_value/step.feature_series read only declared or reused depends_on_features dependencies.
step.option_metric_value/step.option_metric_series read raw anchor-aligned option metrics only: iv_call25, iv_put25, rr25, bf25.
Managed feature columns such as hour, dow, rsi_1, cl_1h, or cf_* remain dependency features when declared through depends_on_features; they are not exposed through step.current(...).
step.for_symbol(symbol) keeps the same contract split: raw frame access through current/series/window, dependency access through feature_value/feature_series.

decision_rules.when: You want labels derived from future trade execution, profitability, TP/SL hits, duration, exit reason, or PnL.
decision_rules.because: python_target only receives current and past context; it cannot look into future candles.

decision_rules.when: You want a custom binary or scalar label computed from forward-looking execution context and the normalized trade outcome.
decision_rules.because: This keeps future-aware logic in the execution runtime while letting compute_label(ctx, outcome) emit the final scalar training label.

decision_rules.when: You want labels derived only from current and historical context available at the anchor candle.
decision_rules.because: compute_target(step) is evaluated row-by-row on the current and past context only.

decision_rules.when: You already have the aligned target as a raw or feature column.
decision_rules.because: column_target is the simplest and most reproducible target mode for precomputed labels.

scenarios.summary: Feature catalog facade loaded.

catalog.name: atr_1
catalog.description: Relative 14-period ATR, expressed as ATR divided by close.

catalog.name: bars_since_expiry
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance since the most recent expiry/delivery event; 0 on the event bar and NaN before the first known event.

catalog.name: bars_since_funding_settlement
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance since the most recent funding settlement; 0 on the settlement bar and NaN before the first known settlement.

catalog.name: bars_until_expiry
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance until the next expiry/delivery event; 0 on the event bar and NaN after the last known future event.

catalog.name: bars_until_funding_settlement
catalog.description: Anchor-bar distance until the next funding settlement; 0 on the settlement bar and NaN after the last known settlement.

catalog.name: cf_atr_regime
catalog.description: ATR regime detector: short/long ATR ratio to identify expanding vs contracting volatility regimes

catalog.name: cf_flow_cumulative_delta_1h
catalog.description: Running signed volume imbalance: cumulative delta over 6 bars on 1h, proxying buy vs sell pressure accumulation

catalog.name: cf_fund_diverge
catalog.description: Funding settlement proximity signal: contrarian momentum signal around funding settlement events using bars_until/bars_since funding settlement

catalog.name: cf_funding_extreme
catalog.description: Contrarian signal around funding settlement events: uses bars_until/bars_since funding settlement to identify pre/post settlement zones, combines with 8-bar return momentum for directional contrarian signals.

catalog.name: cf_funding_momentum_drift
catalog.description: Funding regime momentum overlay: combines existing funding proximity regime (cf_funding_regime) with 4/8-bar price momentum to create directional signals. Approaching funding with positive momentum → mean reversion short; negative → long bounce. Post-settlement follows momentum continuation.

catalog.name: cf_funding_regime
catalog.description: Funding settlement proximity regime: classifies bars into approaching_funding(1), neutral(2), post_settlement(3), at_settlement(4) based on bars_until/bars_since funding settlement.

catalog.name: cf_funding_settlement_gate
catalog.description: Funding settlement proximity gate: fires on settlement bars and the immediately following bar

catalog.name: cf_fwd_vol_regime
catalog.description: Forward 5-bar volatility regime classifier: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high. Uses rolling quantile-based thresholds on forward return volatility.

catalog.name: cf_mean_rev_pressure
catalog.description: Mean-reversion pressure: z-score of close vs 24h rolling VWAP, amplified by RSI divergence filter. Continuous value: positive=overbought pressure, negative=oversold. Divergence between price and RSI momentum amplifies the signal 1.5x.

catalog.name: cf_momentum_divergence_6_24
catalog.description: Cross-lookback momentum divergence: 6-bar ROC minus 24-bar ROC. Positive = short-term momentum accelerating relative to longer-term (trend strengthening). Negative = short-term momentum weakening relative to longer-term (trend exhaustion / transition signal). Captures regime transitions that single-timeframe anchors cannot detect.

catalog.name: cf_range_compression_1h
catalog.description: Ratio of 12-bar ATR to 48-bar ATR on 1h, detecting contraction before expansion moves. Values < 1 = compression, > 1 = expansion.

catalog.name: cf_rv_percentile_48
catalog.description: Rolling 48-bar percentile rank of realized variance — identifies regime extremes

catalog.name: cf_rv_ratio_6_24
catalog.description: Ratio of 6-bar realized variance to 24-bar realized variance on 1h — spikes above 1.5 signal volatility expansion

catalog.name: cf_vol_absorption_score
catalog.description: Volume absorption: volume z-score times (1 - body/range), smoothed over 6 bars. High values = large volume with small body = institutional absorption.

catalog.name: cf_vol_asymmetry_1h
catalog.description: Ratio of upside candle body range to total body range over rolling 6 bars on 1h, capturing directional vol skew from OHLCV alone. Values > 0 = upside dominance, < 0 = downside dominance.

catalog.name: cf_vol_expansion_flag
catalog.description: Vol expansion flag: binary 1 when 5-bar realized vol exceeds 20-bar vol by 1.5x, else 0. Captures sudden vol breakouts.

catalog.name: cf_vol_imbalance_12
catalog.description: Rolling 12-bar volume imbalance: (sum_up_vol - sum_down_vol) / total_vol on 5m bars

catalog.name: cf_vol_price_div
catalog.description: Volume-price divergence: signed rolling price-volume correlation, negative means price trends without volume confirmation

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_compression
catalog.description: 20-bar realized vol percentile rank vs 100-bar window; -1=compression (bottom decile), 1=expansion (top decile), 0=neutral

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_switch
catalog.description: Volatility regime switch detector: binary flag (0/1) based on ATR ratio of 6h vs 48h rolling windows. Value=1 when short-term vol deviates significantly from long-term (ratio >1.2 or <0.8), indicating regime transition.

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_transition
catalog.description: Vol regime transition: 20-bar realized vol percentile minus 5-bar realized vol percentile over 100-bar window. Positive = vol expanding, negative = vol contracting.

catalog.name: cf_vol_regime_vov
catalog.description: Vol-of-vol ratio: 5-bar ATR std / 20-bar ATR std. Values > 1 indicate short-term vol accelerating relative to longer-term.

catalog.name: cf_vol_squeeze_breakout
catalog.description: Detects volatility squeeze release: ratio of 6-bar to 24-bar realized vol crosses above 0.65 after being below 0.55 for 3+ consecutive bars. Binary 1/0 signal.

catalog.name: cf_vol_term_spread
catalog.description: Volatility term-structure regime signal. Ratio of 12-period (1h) to 96-period (8h) rolling return std, minus 1. Values >0 = vol expansion (trending regime), <0 = vol contraction (mean-reverting regime). Captures regime TRANSITIONS, not levels — orthogonal to ATR and single-window vol features.

catalog.name: cf_vwap_dev
catalog.description: Percentage distance of close from 24-period rolling VWAP. Positive = price above VWAP, negative = price below VWAP. Captures mean-reversion regimes.

catalog.name: cf_zscore_reversion
catalog.description: Z-score mean-reversion: 24-bar rolling z-score of close price. Positive values = oversold (buy signal for mean-reversion), negative = overbought.

catalog.name: cl_15m
catalog.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 15m candlestick model. example_args.name: cf_micro_imbalance_1h
example_args.description: Aggregate 5m signed-volume imbalance into one 1h anchor-aligned series.
example_args.code:
import pandas as pd

from app.features.custom.lower_timeframe import aggregate_completed_lower_timeframe

def compute_series(ctx):
    frame = ctx.frame(timeframe='5m')[['open', 'high', 'low', 'close', 'volume', 'timestamp_utc']].copy()
    def _aggregate(lower_frame):
        indexed = lower_frame.set_index('timestamp_utc')
        rng = (indexed['high'] - indexed['low']).replace(0, pd.NA)
        signed_range = (indexed['close'] - indexed['open']) / rng
        signed_volume = signed_range * indexed['volume']
        hourly_signed = signed_volume.resample('1h', label='right', closed='left').sum()
        hourly_volume = indexed['volume'].resample('1h', label='right', closed='left').sum().replace(0, pd.NA)
        return hourly_signed / hourly_volume
    return aggregate_completed_lower_timeframe(
        anchor_frame=ctx.frame(),
        lower_frame=frame,
        lower_timeframe='5m',
        anchor_timeframe='1h',
        feature_name='cf_micro_imbalance_1h',
        aggregator=_aggregate,
    )

Lower-timeframe raw reads such as ctx.frame(timeframe='5m') require required_timeframes=['5m'], explicit timestamp_utc handling, completed-bin aggregation, and alignment to the first honest anchor row.

notes: Declare every external symbol explicitly before calling ctx.for_symbol(...) or step.for_symbol(...).
External feature dependencies are read from already-built managed feature datasets on the anchor timeframe.
Cross-symbol custom features are offline-only in v1 and are rejected by live signal runtime.

guidance: Use managed event-backed columns such as is_funding_settlement, bars_until_funding_settlement, and bars_since_funding_settlement via depends_on_features.
Publish definition first, then build into managed dataset, then reference from snapshot or downstream workflows.
Use features_custom(action='inspect', view='examples') before writing an event-backed feature from scratch.

common_mistakes: Use the facade tool features_custom(action='inspect'|'validate'|'publish'), not hidden primitive tool names.
Prefer compute_series(ctx) for rolling/statistical features; use compute_feature(step) only when logic is genuinely step-dependent.
Apply the cf_ prefix to the feature name, not to the Python function name.
Declare runtime-managed model dependencies through depends_on_models when using step.model_predict(...).
Do not send required_features to features.custom.* tools; the valid fields are depends_on_features and required_columns.
Do not guess model-backed dependencies by hand; inspect models_registry(action='inspect', view='version_detail').feature_runtime_requirements and copy the derived template.
Prefer models_to_feature(action='scaffold') when you want a copy-paste-ready model-backed feature payload instead of wiring fields manually.
Inspect models_registry(action='inspect', view='version_detail') before using a model from a custom feature, especially when deployability or required feature inputs matter.

step.series(column) returns the full history controlled by lookback_bars; its second argument is timeframe (e.g. '4h'), NOT a row count. To get N recent rows use step.window(N) or step.series(column).tail(N).
compute_series(ctx) must return one pandas.Series aligned 1:1 to the anchor frame; returning a scalar, list, or wrong-length series is invalid.

Lower-timeframe reads such as ctx.frame(timeframe='5m') are supported only after declaring required_timeframes=['5m']; raw lower-timeframe reads are not anchor-aligned until you aggregate completed bins and align them to the first honest anchor row.

Custom-feature values persisted on an anchor dataset row are keyed by the anchor row open_time. A value is causal for that row only when every dependency is fully available no later than that anchor open.
Lower-timeframe aggregates must be aligned to the first anchor row where the completed bin is fully usable, not blindly reindexed to ctx.decision_time_ms_series().
compute_feature(step) does not expose ctx-only vectorized fields such as decision_time_ms_series; use compute_series(ctx) for vectorized time-aware logic.

ctx.load(...), ctx.df, ctx.features, and ctx.column(...) are not part of the vectorized contract. Use the explicit compute_series(ctx) accessors from features_custom(action='inspect', view='contract').

Raw event stores are not injected into custom feature code. events_df / ctx.events / step.events are unsupported; use managed event columns such as is_funding_settlement or bars_until_funding_settlement via depends_on_features.

ctx.series(column, timeframe='4h') is a raw higher-timeframe reader and is not anchor-aligned. Use ctx.aligned_series(column, timeframe='4h') when row counts must match the current anchor calculation window.
Cross-symbol access is not implicit. Declare external symbols in cross_symbol_inputs, then use ctx.for_symbol('ETHUSDT') or step.for_symbol('ETHUSDT').
step.current(name) and step.series(name) are raw timeframe accessors only. If a value was declared in depends_on_features, use step.feature_value(name) or step.feature_series(name) instead.
Do not read metrics_mini.csv directly inside published custom features. Use ctx.option_metric_series(name) or step.option_metric_series(name) so live signal runtime can supply the data safely.

Publishing a custom feature does not rebuild managed datasets automatically. After publish, run features_dataset(action='build', columns=['cf_name']) or publish with auto_build_dataset=true when you want an explicit opt-in rebuild.
notes: Custom feature code runs in a trusted subprocess using the server Python environment.
Imports from env6 such as numpy, pandas, and talib are allowed when available to the server runtime.
compute_series(ctx) is the preferred fast path for non-model custom features and should return the full anchor-aligned series in one call.
compute_feature(step) remains supported for backward compatibility and for model-backed or truly step-dependent logic.
Use step.current/series/window/feature_value/feature_series to inspect source and dependency history.
Use ctx.frame/series/aligned_frame/aligned_series/feature_series/option_metric_series/decision_time_ms_series for vectorized custom features.

Custom-feature values persisted on an anchor dataset row are keyed by the anchor row open_time. A value is causal for that row only when every dependency is fully available no later than that anchor open.

Backtest and live entry eligibility may start after the anchor bar closes, but persisted anchor-row feature values must still be honest at the row open unless an explicitly different storage contract is documented.
Use step.model_predict(model_ref, output='default') and step.model_info(model_ref) for runtime-managed trained models.

Read models_registry(action='inspect', view='version_detail') to inspect a model's input_contract, output_contract, deployability_report, artifact summaries, and retrain metadata before wiring it into a feature.
For model-backed features, depends_on_features declares managed feature columns required by runtime inference; required_columns declares raw OHLCV columns such as close, open, high, low, or volume.
Use step.feature_value(name) / step.feature_series(name) for depends_on_features dependencies. step.current(name) reads timeframe frame columns and is not a substitute for dependency features.
Managed features such as hour, dow, rsi_1, cl_1h, and cf_* remain dependency features when declared through depends_on_features; they are not exposed through step.current(name).
Field required_features does not exist in the custom-feature MCP contract. Use depends_on_features instead.
If step.model_predict(...) depends on runtime feature inputs, declare those feature columns in depends_on_features so feature runtime inference can rebuild them.
Vectorized compute_series(ctx) supports model-backed inference through ctx.model_predict_series(...).
ctx.frame(...) and ctx.series(...) are raw readers. They can return non-anchor row counts for higher timeframes.

Lower-timeframe vectorized reads such as ctx.frame(timeframe='5m') are also raw readers. Declare required_timeframes for the source timeframe, convert timestamp_utc into a DatetimeIndex when needed, aggregate completed bins, then align them to the first honest anchor row.
Lower-timeframe aggregates must be aligned to the first anchor row where the completed bin is fully usable, not blindly reindexed to ctx.decision_time_ms_series().
ctx.aligned_frame(...) and ctx.aligned_series(...) are the canonical anchor-aligned accessors for higher-timeframe vectorized logic and always align to the current anchor calculation window.
Use ctx.option_metric_series(name) or step.option_metric_series(name) for raw Deribit option metrics such as iv_call25, iv_put25, rr25, and bf25.
Funding / expiry event families are available through managed event-backed feature columns, not through raw events_df inside custom feature code.
Use ctx.for_symbol(symbol) / step.for_symbol(symbol) for offline cross-symbol reads after declaring the symbol under cross_symbol_inputs.
External-symbol feature_series(...) reads already-built managed feature datasets for that symbol on the anchor timeframe and aligns them back to the anchor frame.
Cross-symbol custom features are offline-only in v1. Live signal runtime and model-backed live feature inference reject them explicitly.

Model-backed validation reuses already-built managed feature columns when available; if validation is slow or times out, prefer lower wait modes and inspect the normalized operation diagnostics returned by features_custom(action='validate')/publish.
When a custom-feature operation fails, inspect the inline logs returned by features_custom(action='validate')/publish before changing the payload.
Network access and filesystem side effects are forbidden by contract even though v1 does not OS-sandbox them.
Historical liquidation-dependent features require a real datasource under artifacts/research_data; validation and publish are blocked when that source is absent.

canonical_block.lower_timeframe_rule: For lower-timeframe raw reads such as 5m->1h, declare required_timeframes=['5m'], aggregate completed bins via timestamp_utc/DatetimeIndex, then align to the first honest anchor row.
canonical_block.cross_symbol_rule: Use string-literal symbols in for_symbol(...) and declare them under cross_symbol_inputs.
canonical_block.row_count_invariant: compute_series(ctx) must return exactly one row per current anchor row.
canonical_block.publish_build_rule: Publishing a custom feature does not activate it in managed datasets. Follow publish with features_dataset(action='build', columns=['cf_name']) or opt into auto_build_dataset=true.
canonical_block.anchor_storage_rule: Custom-feature values persisted on an anchor dataset row are keyed by the anchor row open_time. A value is causal for that row only when every dependency is fully available no later than that anchor open.
canonical_block.causal_lower_timeframe_rule: Lower-timeframe aggregates must be aligned to the first anchor row where the completed bin is fully usable, not blindly reindexed to ctx.decision_time_ms_series().

contract_quick_fixes: Do not send required_features to features.custom.* tools; the valid fields are depends_on_features and required_columns.

Lower-timeframe reads such as ctx.frame(timeframe='5m') are supported only after declaring required_timeframes=['5m']; raw lower-timeframe reads are not anchor-aligned until you aggregate completed bins and align them to the first honest anchor row.
Lower-timeframe aggregates must be aligned to the first anchor row where the completed bin is fully usable, not blindly reindexed to ctx.decision_time_ms_series().
compute_feature(step) does not expose ctx-only vectorized fields such as decision_time_ms_series; use compute_series(ctx) for vectorized time-aware logic.
step.current(name) and step.series(name) are raw timeframe accessors only. If a value was declared in depends_on_features, use step.feature_value(name) or step.feature_series(name) instead.

Publishing a custom feature does not rebuild managed datasets automatically. After publish, run features_dataset(action='build', columns=['cf_name']) or publish with auto_build_dataset=true when you want an explicit opt-in rebuild.
Use ctx.frame/series/aligned_frame/aligned_series/feature_series/option_metric_series/decision_time_ms_series for vectorized custom features.
Use step.feature_value(name) / step.feature_series(name) for depends_on_features dependencies. step.current(name) reads timeframe frame columns and is not a substitute for dependency features.
Managed features such as hour, dow, rsi_1, cl_1h, and cf_* remain dependency features when declared through depends_on_features; they are not exposed through step.current(name).
Field required_features does not exist in the custom-feature MCP contract. Use depends_on_features instead.
Lower-timeframe aggregates must be aligned to the first anchor row where the completed bin is fully usable, not blindly reindexed to ctx.decision_time_ms_series().

target_modes.description: Align a raw or feature source column to the anchor timeframe and use it directly as target.

decision_rules.when: You want labels derived from future trade execution, profitability, TP/SL hits, duration, exit reason, or PnL.
decision_rules.because: python_target only receives current and past context; it cannot look into future candles.

decision_rules.when: You want a custom binary or scalar label computed from forward-looking execution context and the normalized trade outcome.
decision_rules.because: This keeps future-aware logic in the execution runtime while letting compute_label(ctx, outcome) emit the final scalar training label.

decision_rules.when: You want labels derived only from current and historical context available at the anchor candle.
decision_rules.because: compute_target(step) is evaluated row-by-row on the current and past context only.

decision_rules.when: You already have the aligned target as a raw or feature column.
decision_rules.because: column_target is the simplest and most reproducible target mode for precomputed labels.

fields.name: cl_1d
fields.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 1d candlestick model. Class 0: long flat base then sharp upside pump; class 1: monotonic downtrend with acceleration into a weak rebound; class 2: smooth rise into a local top then short pullback; class 3: strong uptrend that accelerates into the end; class 4: wide volatile range with a rounded top then decline.

Monotonic downtrend with acceleration into a weak rebound.

fields.name: cl_1h
fields.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 1h candlestick model. Class 0: sideways to weak down drift followed by a sharp late selloff; class 1: strong early drop then weak bottom-side consolidation; class 2: fast rise in the first half then topping plateau or light pullback; class 3: steady upward acceleration through most of the window, sometimes with a final upside burst.

value_semantics.meaning: Sideways to weak down drift followed by a sharp late selloff.
Strong early drop then weak bottom-side consolidation.
Fast rise in the first half then topping plateau or light pullback.
Steady upward acceleration through most of the window, sometimes with a final upside burst.

fields.name: cl_4h
fields.description: Classifier output aligned to the 1h anchor frame using the 4h candlestick model. Class 0: accumulation/flat base then vertical upside breakout near the end; class 1: rise in the first half then topping and soft drift/distribution; class 2: near-continuous stair-step rally without a deep pullback; class 3: clear downtrend with at most a small rebound late; class 4: volatile arc/hump followed by rollover and decline.

value_semantics.meaning: Accumulation or flat base then vertical upside breakout near the end.

recent_additions.reason: Plan v1 winner: +14.3% PnL ($1496.34 vs $1308.65 baseline), Sharpe 1.456, 330 trades, PF 1.571, max DD ~1.68%. Vol regime transition signal (12/96 period rolling std ratio - 1). Run IDs: standalone 20260407-195135-a6102c78, integrated 20260407-195825-2d03834e.

Orthogonal combo: cf_vol_term_spread regime filter only (PROMOTED feature). Beats baseline Sharpe 1.46 vs 1.06 (+38%), PnL $1496 vs $1309 (+14.3%), trades 330 vs 498 (more selective), max DD 1.68% vs 2.44% (-31%). cf_funding_extreme and cf_return_skew were REJECT in individual tests. Signal ownership clean: long 50.5% PnL, short 49.5%. No cannibalization.

ETAP 3: Vol term spread feature improves PnL +14.3% ($1496 vs $1309), Sharpe +38% (1.46 vs 1.06), drawdown -31% (1.68% vs 2.44%). Long PnL +84% improvement. Feature cf_vol_term_spread filters vol contraction regimes effectively.
Baseline v1: Sharpe 1.056, +13.1%, 498 trades, max DD 2.44%, PF 1.305. Full history 2021-03 to 2026-03.
